From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

In session from 1997 to 2002

The **15th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party** was in session from 1997 to 2002. The [14th Central Committee](/wiki/14th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "14th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party") preceded it. It was followed by the [16th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/16th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "16th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party"). This was the first Central Committee that current [CCP general secretary](/wiki/General_Secretary_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party") [Xi Jinping](/wiki/Xi_Jinping "Xi Jinping") was elected to, as an alternative member.

It elected the [15th Politburo of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/15th_Politburo_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "15th Politburo of the Chinese Communist Party") in 1997. Plenary sessions were held by the [politburo](/wiki/Politburo_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "Politburo of the Chinese Communist Party").

## Brief chronology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=15th_Central_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Brief chronology")]

1. *1st Plenary Session*
   * Date: September 19, 1997
   * Location: [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing")
   * Significance: [Jiang Zemin](/wiki/Jiang_Zemin "Jiang Zemin") was re-appointed [General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party](/wiki/General_Secretary_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party") and [Chairman of the Central Military Commission](/wiki/Chairman_of_the_Central_Military_Commission_(China) "Chairman of the Central Military Commission (China)"). A 24-members [Politburo](/wiki/Politburo_of_the_Communist_Party_of_China "Politburo of the Communist Party of China"), a 7-members [Politburo Standing Committee](/wiki/Politburo_Standing_Committee_of_the_Chinese_Communist_Party "Politburo Standing Committee of the Chinese Communist Party") and a 7-members [Secretariat](/wiki/Secretariat_of_the_Communist_Party_of_China "Secretariat of the Communist Party of China") were elected.
2. *2nd Plenary Session*
   * Date: February 25–26, 1998
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: The meeting approved lists of nominees for top posts of the [9th National People's Congress](/wiki/9th_National_People%27s_Congress "9th National People's Congress") and the [9th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference](/wiki/Chinese_People%27s_Political_Consultative_Conference "Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference") and a proposal for reforming the [State Council](/wiki/State_Council_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China "State Council of the People's Republic of China").
3. *3rd Plenary Session*
   * Date: October 12–14, 1998
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: The meeting reviewed the previous 20 years of economic reform and set the goal to establish new rural towns according to [socialism with Chinese characteristics](/wiki/Socialism_with_Chinese_characteristics "Socialism with Chinese characteristics") by 2010.
4. *4th Plenary Session*
   * Date: September 19–22, 1999
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: The reform of the [state-owned enterprises](/wiki/State-owned_enterprises_of_China "State-owned enterprises of China") was launched aiming at establishing a *"modern enterprise system"*. It emphasized that state ownership should be dominant in "important industries and key areas" and in "backbone enterprises in important industries".[[1]](#cite_note-:Leutert-1): 53 [Hu Jintao](/wiki/Hu_Jintao "Hu Jintao") was appointed vice-chairman of the Central Military Commission.
5. *5th Plenary Session*
   * Date: October 9–11, 2000
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: Premier [Zhu Rongji](/wiki/Zhu_Rongji "Zhu Rongji") delivered a report on the guidelines for 10th Five-Year Plan, proclaiming modernization, opening up and technological progress as its basic goals. The meeting also decided upon China's accession to the [World Trade Organization](/wiki/World_Trade_Organization "World Trade Organization") and urged the Party to apply Jiang Zemin's [Three Represents](/wiki/Three_Represents "Three Represents"), exposed by the Party General Secretary in February of the same year. The plan for [China's western development](/wiki/China_Western_Development "China Western Development") was formally put forward during the Fifth Plenary Session.[[2]](#cite_note-:02-2) : 401
6. *6th Plenary Session*
   * Date: September 24–26, 2001
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: The meeting celebrated the 80th anniversary of the establishment of the CCP, with General Secretary Jiang Zeming speaking about it, and adopted a *Decision of the CCP Central Committee to Strengthen and Improve the Party's Work Style* based on the Three Represents, allowing capitalists to join the Party.
7. *7th Plenary Session*
   * Date: November 3–5, 2002
   * Location: Beijing
   * Significance: Preparations for the Party's 16th National Congress were made. The meeting proclaimed Deng Xiaoping Theory and the Three Represents as the guideline for China *"in the new century and new stage of reform and opening up and socialist modernization"*.
